sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Stade Rennais are in excellent form, having won 4 of their last 5 matches, including convincing victories against lower-tier teams and a strong Ligue 1 win over Lille. Le Havre, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing only 1 win in their last 5 matches with losses and a draw against weaker opposition, highlighting defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Stade Rennais have dominated Le Havre, remaining unbeaten with a 5-1 thrashing in April 2025 and a 2-2 draw in October 2025. Rennais have consistently scored first and maintained an attacking edge, with both teams often scoring in these fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Stade Rennais, sitting 6th in Ligue 1 with 30 points, boast a strong attack (29 goals scored, 5th in the league) and solid defensive stats, complemented by high duel-winning percentages. Le Havre, positioned 13th with 18 points, have struggled offensively (15 goals scored) and defensively, conceding 23 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Rennais' in-form players like Quentin Merlin and Mousa Tamari could exploit Le Havre's shaky defense, while Esteban Lepaul’s 9 goals make him a constant threat. Le Havre are missing key players like Abdoulaye Touré and Arouna Sangante, weakening their midfield and defense further.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Rennais’ superior possession (51% average) and ability to create big chances (37 this season) suggest they’ll control the game at Roazhon Park. Le Havre’s tendency to concede first (5/7 matches) and their low scoring output could see them struggle against Rennais’ pressing and final-third efficiency.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 55% home win probability, combined with Rennais’ form and historical dominance, they are clear favorites. Le Havre’s recent struggles and missing players make an upset unlikely, though they might nick a goal given past head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Stade Rennais and Le Havre at Roazhon Park ended in a goalless draw, with neither side able to break the deadlock. Both teams showed moments of promise but lacked the clinical edge needed to convert their opportunities. Le Havre appeared slightly more dominant in possession and created a big chance, though they missed it. Stade Rennais, playing at home, managed a few shots on target but struggled to create clear-cut openings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Le Havre controlled more of the ball in the first half and showed greater intent in the final third with more entries and penalty area penetrations. Their higher expected goals suggest they were closer to scoring, though their missed big chance highlights a need for better finishing. Stade Rennais, on the other hand, were competitive in duels, particularly in the air, but their lower possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas indicate they were less threatening overall. Defensively, both teams held firm, with no major errors leading to goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Stade Rennais, Esteban Lepaul stood out with a shot on target and a decent individual performance, showing potential to influence the game in the second half. Przemysław Frankowski also contributed defensively and offensively with key passes and tackles. On Le Havre's side, Rassoul Ndiaye was a creative force in midfield, creating a big chance and delivering key passes. Ayumu Seko had a notable opportunity but missed a big chance, which could have put his team ahead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Stade Rennais started strongly with consistent attacking spells in the early minutes, pushing forward with intent. However, Le Havre gradually took control as the half progressed, with sustained periods of pressure in the middle and latter stages. Their attacking surges were more pronounced towards the end of the half, though they couldn't capitalize. Stade Rennais managed a late push before the whistle, but it wasn't enough to shift the balance significantly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Le Havre's edge in possession and higher expected goals in the first half, they are likely to continue pressing for an opener in the second half. Stade Rennais will need to capitalize on home advantage and improve their final third play to create better chances. Both teams have shown defensive resilience, so breaking the deadlock might come down to a moment of individual brilliance or a tactical adjustment. With the game still finely poised, substitutions could play a key role in tilting the balance.
